Place the beetroot cubes in a salad bowl, add the toasted walnuts, and crumble the feta cheese over the top.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to combine all ingredients.
Garnish with fresh parsley leaves before serving.

name: Beignets
description: Beignets are deep-fried French doughnuts, popular in New Orleans, known for their light, airy texture and dusting of powdered sugar.
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve the yeast in warm water and let it activate for about 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, and a pinch of salt.
Add the activated yeast mixture, egg, and half of the milk to the dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
Pour in the melted butter and the remaining milk, then knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for about 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it doubles in size.
Once risen, punch down the dough and roll it out on a floured surface to about 1/4 inch thickness.
Cut the dough into 2-inch squares or rectangles.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep fryer or heavy pot to 180°C (350°F).
Carefully fry the dough pieces in batches until they puff up and become golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Use a slotted spoon to remove the beignets from the oil and drain them on paper towels.
While still warm, generously dust the beignets with powdered sugar and serve immediately.

name: Belgian Style Waffles
description: Belgian style waffles are light, crispy, and fluffy waffles made with a batter that incorporates whipped egg whites for extra volume. They are often served with sweet toppings like fruit and syrup.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, separate the eggs. Beat the egg yolks and mix in the milk and melted butter.
Combine the wet ingredients with the dry ingredients and mix until just blended.
In a clean bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the waffle batter to keep it light and fluffy.
Preheat the waffle iron and lightly grease it with butter or cooking spray.
Pour the batter onto the hot waffle iron, spreading evenly, and cook until golden brown and crisp, usually about 4 to 5 minutes.
Remove the waffles carefully and keep warm. Repeat with the remaining batter.
Serve the waffles hot with toppings like fresh fruit, whipped cream, or maple syrup as desired.

name: Belgian-style Waffles
description: Belgian-style waffles are thick, crispy on the outside and light on the inside, traditionally served with various toppings for a delicious breakfast treat.
instructions: Preheat a waffle iron according to the manufacturer's instructions.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In another bowl, separate the egg yolks from the egg whites.
Add the egg yolks, milk, melted butter, and vanilla extract to the dry ingredients and mix until smooth.
In a clean bowl, beat the egg whites with an electric mixer until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter, being careful not to deflate them.
Lightly grease the waffle iron with butter or oil.
Pour the batter onto the preheated waffle iron, using enough to cover the surface but not overflow.
Close the waffle iron and cook until the waffles are golden brown and crisp, about 4-5 minutes.
Carefully remove the waffles and serve immediately with your choice of toppings such as fresh fruit, whipped cream, or syrup.

name: Bellini (Peach and Prosecco Cocktail)
description: Bellini is a classic Italian cocktail from Venice that combines fresh peach puree with sparkling Prosecco, known for its light and refreshing taste.
instructions: Chill the Prosecco and peach puree before preparing the cocktail.
Peel and pit the peach, then blend it until smooth to create fresh peach puree.
Pour 60 ml of peach puree into a chilled champagne flute.
Slowly add 90 ml of chilled Prosecco to the flute to prevent excessive foaming.
Gently stir the mixture to combine the peach puree and Prosecco evenly.
Serve immediately while cold and enjoy.

name: Bellini Peach (Peach Bellini)
description: The Peach Bellini is a classic Italian cocktail made with fresh peach puree and sparkling wine, known for its refreshing and fruity flavor, typically served as an elegant aperitif.
instructions: Chill the sparkling wine or prosecco thoroughly in the refrigerator for at least a few hours before preparation.
Peel the peach, remove the pit, and cut the fruit into small slices.
Place the peach slices into a blender and blend until smooth to make peach puree.
Optional: Strain the peach puree through a fine sieve to remove any pulp for a smoother texture.
Add a small amount of peach puree into a chilled champagne flute (about 30 ml).
Slowly pour chilled sparkling wine or prosecco over the peach puree into the flute, filling it to the top.
Gently stir the mixture to combine without losing the bubbles.
Serve immediately as a refreshing and elegant cocktail.

name: Bellini Peach Cocktail
description: Bellini Peach is an iconic Italian cocktail made with fresh peach puree and chilled prosecco, perfect for a refreshing and elegant drink.
instructions: Chill the prosecco or sparkling wine in the refrigerator beforehand.
Peel the peach by blanching it briefly in boiling water, then placing it in ice water to loosen the skin.
Remove the skin and pit from the peach, then cut the flesh into small pieces.
Place the peach pieces in a blender and puree until smooth.
Strain the peach puree through a fine mesh sieve to remove any solids for a smoother texture.
Pour about one-third chilled peach puree into a chilled champagne flute.
Slowly top up the flute with chilled prosecco or sparkling wine, stirring gently to combine.
Serve immediately as a refreshing cocktail.

name: Beni Shoga (Red Pickled Ginger)
description: Beni Shoga is a traditional Japanese condiment made from thinly sliced ginger pickled in red shiso vinegar, offering a characteristic bright red color and sharp flavor that complements many dishes.
instructions: Slice fresh ginger into thin julienne strips about 5 cm long.
Mix red shiso leaves with salt in a bowl to create the characteristic red color and flavor.
Combine sliced ginger with salted red shiso leaves and let it sit for about 30 minutes.
In a separate pot, bring rice vinegar, sugar, and salt to a boil and stir until dissolved.
Pour the hot vinegar mixture over the ginger and shiso mix, ensuring the ginger is fully submerged.
Transfer the mixture to a clean jar, cover, and let it pickle at room temperature for 3 to 5 days for best flavor.
After pickling, refrigerate the Beni Shoga; it can be consumed immediately but flavor improves after a week.
Use as a condiment for various Japanese dishes, especially in okonomiyaki, takoyaki, or with rice dishes.

name: Beniimo Tart (Purple Sweet Potato Tart)
description: Beniimo Tart is a Japanese-inspired dessert showcasing the naturally vibrant purple sweet potato in a creamy, smooth tart filling within a buttery crust.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
Prepare the tart crust by mixing flour, sugar, and butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Add egg yolk and water to the crust mixture and combine until a dough forms. Wrap in plastic and chill for 30 minutes.
Roll out the chilled dough and fit it into a tart pan. Prick the base with a fork and bake for 15 minutes until lightly golden.
Peel and boil purple sweet potato until soft, then mash until smooth.
In a bowl, mix the mashed purple sweet potato with sugar, heavy cream, egg, melted butter, and vanilla extract until well combined.
Pour the sweet potato filling into the pre-baked tart crust.
Bake the tart for 25-30 minutes at 180°C until the filling is set and slightly browned on top.
Allow the tart to cool before serving.

name: Beppu Jigoku Mushi (Hell-steamed Food)
description: Beppu Jigoku Mushi is a traditional Japanese dish from Beppu, where various fresh ingredients like seafood, eggs, and vegetables are steamed using natural hot spring steam, resulting in a healthy and gently cooked meal.
instructions: Fill a large pot or steamer with water and bring it to a boil to generate steam.
Place the food items suitable for steaming (such as seafood, vegetables, and eggs) into a steamer basket or metal container that can withstand direct steam heat.
Once the water is boiling, carefully place the steamer basket or container into the pot so the steam cooks the food without submerging it in water.
Cover the pot with a tight-fitting lid and cook the food using the steam for about 10 to 20 minutes, depending on the ingredients' size and type.
Check the doneness by piercing with a fork or skewer; seafood should be opaque and vegetables tender but firm.
Carefully remove the steamer basket or container from the pot to avoid burns from steam.
Serve the steamed food immediately with dipping sauces such as soy sauce or ponzu, and garnish with optional fresh herbs or lemon wedges if desired.

name: Bercy Sauce
description: Bercy Sauce is a classic French sauce made with shallots, white wine, fish stock, and butter, traditionally served with fish dishes for a rich and elegant flavor.
instructions: Mince the shallot finely.
In a saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the minced shallot to the melted butter and sauté until translucent and soft, but not browned.
Pour the white wine into the pan with the shallots and increase the heat to bring to a boil.
Allow the wine to reduce by about half to concentrate the flavors.
Add the fish stock to the pan and continue to simmer until the sauce reduces to the desired consistency, about 5 to 7 minutes.
Lower the heat and whisk in the cold butter piece by piece to emulsify the sauce, creating a smooth and velvety texture.
Season with salt and white pepper to taste.
Fold in the chopped fresh parsley just before serving to add freshness and color.

name: Berenjenas a La Miel (Eggplant with Honey)
description: Berenjenas a la Miel is a traditional Spanish dish featuring fried eggplant slices drizzled with sweet honey, combining savory and sweet flavors for a delightful appetizer or side dish.
instructions: Cut the eggplant into 1 cm thick slices and soak them in salted water for 30 minutes to remove bitterness.
Drain the eggplant slices and pat them dry with a clean towel.
Heat olive oil in a frying pan over medium heat.
Dredge each eggplant slice lightly in flour, shaking off excess flour.
Fry the eggplant slices in the hot oil until golden brown on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Remove the fried eggplant slices and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Arrange the fried eggplant slices on a serving plate.
Drizzle honey generously over the top of the eggplant slices while still warm.
Optionally, sprinkle a pinch of cinnamon over the honey-drizzled eggplant.
Serve warm as a sweet and savory appetizer or side dish.

name: Bergama Style Meatball (Bergama Koefte)
description: Bergama Style Meatball, known as Bergama Koefte, is a traditional Turkish dish featuring seasoned ground beef and lamb meatballs cooked in a rich tomato and pepper sauce, originating from the Bergama region.
instructions: Soak the bread in water until softened, then squeeze out excess water and crumble it into a large mixing bowl.
Add ground beef and ground lamb to the bowl along with finely chopped onion, minced garlic, egg, and finely chopped parsley.
Season the mixture with salt, black pepper, cumin, and paprika, then mix thoroughly using your hands until well combined and uniform in texture.
Shape the mixture into small oval meatballs, about the size of a walnut, and place them on a tray.
Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium heat and fry the meatballs in batches, turning occasionally, until they are browned and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
In a separate pan, melt butter and sauté finely chopped green and red bell peppers until soft.
Add crushed tomatoes to the peppers and simmer for 10 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper to create a sauce.
Place the fried meatballs into the tomato and pepper sauce, cover, and simmer gently for 10 minutes to absorb the flavors.
Serve the Bergama style meatballs hot, garnished with fresh parsley, accompanied by rice or bread as desired.

name: Berry Potato Pie
description: Berry potato pie is a unique dessert combining creamy mashed potatoes and sweet mixed berries baked inside a flaky pie crust, creating a comforting and flavorful treat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
Peel and cut potatoes into chunks, then boil in salted water until tender. Drain and mash with butter and a pinch of salt. Set aside to cool.
In a mixing bowl, combine mixed berries, sugar, and cornstarch; toss gently to coat.
Roll out pie crust and place it in a pie dish, trimming the edges.
Spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the crust as the base layer.
Pour the berry mixture over the mashed potatoes, spreading it evenly.
Dot small pieces of butter on top of the berries.
Cover the pie with a second rolled-out crust, sealing the edges and making a few slits on top to vent.
Brush the top crust with a beaten egg for shine.
Bake the pie for 45-50 minutes, until the crust is golden and the filling is bubbling.
Let the pie cool before serving to set the filling.

name: Berza Gaditana (Cadiz Cabbage Stew)
description: Berza Gaditana is a traditional hearty cabbage stew from Cadiz, Spain, combining pork meats, vegetables, and chickpeas for a flavorful and warming dish.
instructions: In a large pot, bring water to a boil and add the pork shoulder, pork ribs, chorizo, and bacon. Simmer gently to create a rich broth, skimming off any foam.
After 45 minutes, add the garbanzo beans and cook until tender, about 1 hour more.
Add the cabbage, potato, carrot, green bean, and pumpkin to the pot. Continue to cook until the vegetables are soft, about 30 minutes.
Season the stew with salt and black pepper to taste.
Remove the meats from the stew and cut the pork shoulder and ribs into pieces. Slice the chorizo and bacon.
Return the meats to the pot to heat through.
Serve the stew hot, making sure each portion has a good mix of meat, vegetables, and beans.

name: Berza Gaditana (Traditional Cadiz-style Stew)
description: Berza Gaditana is a traditional hearty stew from Cadiz, Spain, featuring chickpeas, pork, chicken, and fresh vegetables simmered slowly to develop rich and comforting flavors.
instructions: Soak the chickpeas in water overnight to soften them.
In a large pot, add the soaked chickpeas, pork belly, pork rib, chicken leg, and water.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for about 1.5 to 2 hours, skimming any foam that forms on the surface.
Add the chopped cabbage, peeled and chopped potato, green beans, carrot, garlic cloves, and salt to the pot.
Simmer for another 30 to 40 minutes until all ingredients are tender and flavors meld.
Remove the meat and bones from the pot; shred the meat and discard bones and excess fat.
Return the shredded meat to the pot and stir well.
Adjust seasoning with salt to taste, and simmer for an additional 10 minutes before serving.
Serve hot in bowls, ensuring each serving has a good mix of meat, chickpeas, and vegetables.

name: Beso De ángel (Angel's Kiss Cake)
description: Beso de Ángel (Angel's Kiss Cake) is a light and airy Filipino chiffon cake soaked in sweet syrup and topped with toasted meringue, offering a delicate and sweet finish. Its soft texture combined with the toasted meringue makes it a popular festive dessert.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease and flour a bundt or tube pan.
In a mixing bowl, combine the sugar and egg yolks. Beat until the mixture lightens in color and becomes fluffy.
Sift the cake flour and baking powder together. Gradually fold this dry mixture into the egg yolk mixture, alternating with vegetable oil and orange juice, mixing gently to maintain airiness.
In a separate clean bowl, beat the egg whites with a pinch of salt until soft peaks form, then gradually add sugar and continue beating until stiff peaks are achieved.
Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the batter in three parts, ensuring not to deflate the mixture.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for about 40 to 45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
While the cake is baking, prepare the syrup by combining sugar and water in a saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer until the sugar dissolves completely.
When the cake is done, remove it from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes in the pan, then invert onto a serving plate.
Brush the warm cake generously with the prepared syrup to keep it moist.
For the meringue topping, whip egg whites with sugar over a double boiler until warm, then beat until stiff and glossy peaks form.
Spread or pipe the meringue over the cooled cake.
Use a kitchen torch to lightly brown the meringue, giving it a toasted appearance. Alternatively, you can place it under a broiler for a brief moment, watching carefully to prevent burning.
Chill the cake before serving to set the meringue and enhance flavors.

name: Besos (Kiss Pastries)
description: Besos (Kiss Pastries) are delicate Argentinian cookies made of rich cocoa dough sandwiched with dulce de leche, offering a sweet, tender treat beloved in Latin America.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
Add the egg and vanilla extract, and mix well.
Sift in the flour and cocoa powder, and gently fold into the butter mixture until a soft dough forms.
Divide the dough in half.
Place one half of the dough between two sheets of parchment paper and roll out to a thin layer (about 3mm thick).
Repeat with the other half of the dough.
Cut out matching shapes (small circles or hearts) from each dough half using a cookie cutter.
Place half of the cutouts on a baking tray lined with parchment paper.
Bake these base cookies for 8 to 10 minutes until firm but not browned, then cool completely.
On the remaining cutouts, spread a small amount of dulce de leche or caramel spread.
Sandwich the bases with the dulce de leche topped cookies, pressing gently to make 'kisses'.
Dust the finished pastries with powdered sugar before serving.

name: Besugo Al Horno (Baked Sea Bream)
description: Besugo al Horno is a traditional Spanish dish featuring whole sea bream baked with garlic, parsley, lemon, and olive oil, delivering a flavorful and moist fish with roasted potatoes.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (390°F).
Clean the sea bream, remove scales and innards, and pat it dry with paper towels.
Make slashes on both sides of the fish to allow flavors to penetrate.
Season the fish inside and out with salt and black pepper.
Place the fish on a roasting tray lined with sliced potato rounds.
Drizzle olive oil over the fish and potatoes.
Stuff the cavity with garlic cloves, fresh parsley sprigs, and lemon slices.
Arrange additional lemon slices and parsley on top of the fish.
Add white wine to the tray to keep the fish moist during baking.
Roast the fish in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes or until the flesh is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove from the oven, drizzle with a little more olive oil, and serve hot with the roasted potatoes and lemon wedges.

name: Besugo De Nochebuena (Christmas Eve Bream)
description: Besugo de Nochebuena is a traditional Spanish Christmas Eve dish featuring whole bream fish roasted with garlic, parsley, and lemon, then served with a fragrant white wine sauce. It is cherished for its simple, fresh flavors and festive presentation.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
Clean and scale the whole bream, then pat it dry with paper towels.
Make three diagonal cuts on each side of the fish to allow flavors to penetrate.
Rub the fish inside and out with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Stuff the cavity of the fish with fresh parsley sprigs and lemon slices.
Place the fish on a baking tray lined with parchment paper or lightly greased with olive oil.
Drizzle olive oil over the fish and sprinkle minced garlic evenly on top.
Bake the fish in the preheated oven for about 25-30 minutes, or until the flesh is opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
While the fish is baking, prepare the sauce by heating a tablespoon of olive oil in a small pan.
Add minced garlic and sauté until fragrant and golden, then add white wine and allow it to reduce by half.
Remove from heat and stir in chopped fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Once the fish is cooked, transfer it to a serving platter and drizzle the garlic, parsley, and white wine sauce over the top.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional lemon wedges and parsley if desired.

name: Besugo a La Madrilena (Madrid Sea Bream)
description: Besugo a la Madrilena is a traditional Madrid-style baked sea bream dish featuring fresh seafood, garlic, and tomatoes, known for its rich flavors and Mediterranean roots.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (390°F).
Clean the sea bream, removing scales and innards, then rinse and pat dry.
Make shallow diagonal cuts on both sides of the fish to help it cook evenly and absorb the flavors.
Season the sea bream inside and out with salt and pepper.
In a roasting pan, place the cleaned clams and peeled shrimp as the base.
Place the sea bream on top of the seafood in the pan.
Scatter the peeled garlic cloves and whole peeled tomatoes around the fish.
Drizzle olive oil generously over the fish and seafood.
Add a few sprigs of fresh parsley around for aroma.
Place the roasting pan in the oven and bake for about 25-30 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the seafood is tender.
Once cooked, carefully remove the fish and seafood onto a serving dish.
Spoon the cooked tomatoes, garlic, and pan juices over the fish.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot, optionally with steamed vegetables or potatoes on the side.

name: Beyaz Pilav (Fluffy White Rice)
description: Beyaz Pilav is a traditional Turkish fluffy white rice cooked in butter and broth for rich flavor and perfect texture.
instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear to remove excess starch.
Soak the rice in cold water for about 20 minutes, then drain thoroughly.
Melt the butter and olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.
Add the drained rice to the saucepan and gently sauté, stirring constantly, until each grain is coated and slightly translucent, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the hot chicken broth and a pinch of salt to the rice mixture, then bring to a boil.
Once boiling, reduce heat to low and cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer gently for 15-18 minutes, or until all the liquid is absorbed and the rice is tender.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it stand, covered, for an additional 10 minutes to allow the rice to steam.
Fluff the rice gently with a fork before serving.

name: Beyond Meat Plant Burger
description: A hearty and savory plant-based burger using Beyond Meat's plant burger patty, perfect for vegan and meat-free meals.
instructions: Preheat a grill or skillet over medium heat.
Remove the Beyond Meat plant burger patties from the packaging and let them thaw if frozen.
Lightly oil the grill or skillet to prevent sticking.
Place the patties on the grill or skillet.
Cook for about 3 to 4 minutes on one side until the edges start to brown and grill marks form.
Flip the patties and cook for an additional 3 to 4 minutes on the other side until thoroughly heated and cooked through.
If desired, place a slice of vegan cheese on top of each patty during the last minute of cooking to melt.
Toast the hamburger buns lightly on the grill or skillet.
Assemble the burger by placing the cooked patty on the bottom bun, then adding lettuce, tomato slices, vegan mayo, ketchup, mustard, and pickles as preferred.
Cover with the top bun and serve immediately.

name: Beyran Soup (Lamb Broth Gaziantep)
description: Beyran is a traditional spicy lamb broth soup from Gaziantep, Turkey, known for its rich flavor and warming qualities.
instructions: Rinse the lamb bones and meat pieces under cold running water.
Place the lamb bones and lamb meat in a large pot and add enough water to cover them.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium and simmer for about 2 hours, skimming off any foam that forms on the surface to keep the broth clear.
While the broth is simmering, peel and finely chop the garlic.
After 2 hours, remove the bones and meat from the broth. Shred the meat into small pieces and set aside.
Strain the broth through a fine sieve or cheesecloth into another pot to ensure clarity.
Return the broth to the stove and bring it to a gentle boil again.
In a separate small pan, heat the olive oil and fry the chopped garlic until fragrant but not browned.
Add the garlic and olive oil mixture to the boiling broth.
Add red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper to taste into the broth. Adjust seasoning as needed.
Add the shredded lamb meat back into the broth and let it simmer for another 10 minutes to combine flavors.
In a small bowl, beat the egg yolk with the vinegar until smooth.
Temper the egg yolk mixture by slowly adding some hot broth to it while stirring constantly, then pour it back into the pot, stirring continuously to avoid curdling.
Serve the hot Beyran soup immediately in bowls, garnished with chopped fresh parsley and a wedge of lemon to squeeze over if desired.

name: Beyti Kebab Wrapped in Lavash
description: Beyti Kebab Wrapped in Lavash is a traditional Turkish dish featuring seasoned grilled lamb kebabs wrapped in soft lavash bread and topped with a rich tomato sauce. This flavorful dish is popular street food and perfect for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Soak the lamb mince in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
In a bowl, combine lamb mince, finely chopped onion, garlic, parsley, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes. Mix well until the mixture is homogeneous.
Divide the mixture into equal portions and shape each into elongated sausages, about 10 cm long.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat and brush with olive oil.
Grill the kebabs, turning occasionally, until evenly browned and cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Warm the lavash bread slightly to make it pliable.
Place a grilled kebab on a piece of lavash and wrap it tightly around the kebab, folding in the edges to secure.
Arrange the wrapped kebabs on a serving plate.
Prepare the tomato sauce by heating olive oil in a saucepan, adding crushed garlic and sautéing briefly.
Add tomato puree, paprika, salt, and sugar to the pan and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour the tomato sauce over the wrapped kebabs.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve immediately.

name: Bezelye Corbasi (Pea Soup)
description: Bezelye Çorbası is a traditional Turkish pea soup made from split peas and vegetables, blended into a smooth, nourishing, and hearty soup.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion, carrot, and potato to the pot and sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the rinsed split peas and stir for a minute with the vegetables.
Pour in the water or chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 40-50 minutes or until peas and vegetables are tender.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Using a hand blender or regular blender, puree the soup until smooth and creamy.
Return the soup to the pot and season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Heat through gently, adjusting consistency with additional water or broth if needed.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh parsley or mint if desired.

name: Bibeleskaes (Alsatian Fresh Cheese)
description: Bibeleskaes is a traditional fresh cheese from Alsace, made by naturally curdling milk and mixing the soft cheese with fresh herbs for a flavorful spread.
instructions: Bring a pot of milk to a gentle simmer, avoiding boiling.
Add a small amount of rennet or natural starter culture to the warm milk to encourage curdling, then cover and let it rest at room temperature for several hours until curds form.
Once the milk has curdled, use a slotted spoon or cheesecloth to gently transfer the curds into a colander lined with cheesecloth.
Allow the curds to drain for several hours or overnight in the refrigerator to remove whey, forming a soft fresh cheese.
Transfer the drained curds to a bowl and season with salt and finely chopped fresh chives, dill, or parsley to taste.
Serve chilled as a spread on bread or alongside potatoes and raw vegetables.

name: Biber Dolmasi (Cold Stuffed Pepper)
description: Biber Dolmasi is a traditional Turkish dish of small peppers stuffed with a flavorful rice and herb mixture, served cold as a refreshing appetizer.
instructions: Wash the small peppers thoroughly and carefully remove their tops and seeds, keeping the peppers intact for stuffing.
In a mixing bowl, combine rice, finely chopped onion, chopped fresh parsley, chopped fresh dill, chopped fresh mint, and pine nut and currant if using.
Add olive oil, salt, pepper, and a pinch of cinnamon to the rice mixture and mix well.
Stuff each pepper gently with the rice mixture, filling them almost to the top but allowing room for the rice to expand during cooking.
Place the stuffed peppers upright in a pot, packed tightly together to prevent movement during cooking.
Pour water over the peppers until it covers them and add a drizzle of olive oil and a pinch of salt.
Cover the pot with a plate to keep the peppers from moving and cook over low heat until the rice inside is tender, about 30-40 minutes.
Remove from heat and allow to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for a few hours.
Serve the chilled stuffed peppers with a drizzle of olive oil and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice on top, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.

name: Biber Kizartma (Fried Peppers)
description: Biber Kizartma is a traditional Turkish side dish featuring fried peppers served with garlic and lemon, celebrated for its simplicity and vibrant flavors.
instructions: Wash the peppers thoroughly and pat them dry with a kitchen towel.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat until hot but not smoking.
Fry the whole peppers in batches, turning occasionally, until their skins are blistered and they are softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Use a slotted spoon to remove the peppers and place them on paper towels to drain excess oil.
Once slightly cooled, transfer the peppers to a serving dish.
Crush the garlic cloves and sprinkle them over the hot peppers.
Drizzle the lemon juice evenly on the peppers and season with salt to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.

name: Bicak Arasi
description: Bicak Arasi is a traditional Gaziantep knife-cut pastry made by frying thin strips of dough until crispy, often enjoyed as a sweet snack with powdered sugar or honey.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine all-purpose flour and salt.
Gradually add lukewarm water and knead until a smooth, elastic dough forms, about 10 minutes.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into small equal portions and roll each portion into thin sheets about 2 mm thick.
Sprinkle some all-purpose flour on the dough sheets and stack them up.
Using a sharp knife, cut the stacked dough sheets into thin strips resembling noodles or ribbons.
Heat vegetable oil in a deep frying pan over medium heat.
Carefully drop the knife-cut dough strips into the hot oil and fry until they turn golden brown and crispy, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Remove the fried pastry with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil.
Optionally, sprinkle powdered sugar or drizzle honey over the crispy pastries before serving.

name: Bici Bici Starch Jelly Dessert (Adana)
description: Bici Bici is a traditional Turkish dessert from Adana featuring delicate starch jelly cubes served chilled with rose water, crushed ice, and pistachios, offering a refreshing summer treat.
instructions: In a saucepan, combine water, sugar, and cornstarch. Stir well until the cornstarch is fully dissolved to avoid lumps.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and continuously stir the mixture until it thickens and becomes a translucent jelly-like consistency.
Remove the thickened mixture from heat and pour it into a shallow dish. Spread it evenly and let it cool to room temperature.
Once cooled, refrigerate the jelly for at least 2 hours until it is firm and fully set.
Cut the chilled jelly into small cubes and place them into serving glasses or bowls.
Sprinkle crushed ice over the jelly cubes to make the dessert refreshing and cool.
Pour rose water evenly over the jelly and ice to infuse a delicate floral aroma.
Top with finely crushed roasted pistachios for texture and flavor before serving.

name: Bienmesabe (Canary Almond Cream)
description: Bienmesabe is a traditional dessert from the Canary Islands made with almond cream, sugar syrup, coconut milk, and aromatics, resulting in a sweet and rich treat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
Spread the blanched almond over a baking tray and toast them in the oven until golden and fragrant, about 10 minutes. Remove and let cool.
In a blender or food processor, grind the toasted almonds until fine but not oily.
In a saucepan, combine the sugar and water. Heat over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves to form a syrup.
Add the ground almonds, coconut milk, lemon zest, and cinnamon to the syrup. Stir continuously to combine and cook gently for 10 minutes until the mixture thickens slightly.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly, then stir in the egg yolks one at a time to enrich the cream, mixing well after each addition.
Return the mixture to low heat and cook gently while stirring until it thickens to a custard-like consistency, about 5 minutes. Do not boil, to avoid curdling the egg yolks.
Pour the Bienmesabe into serving dishes. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving.
Optionally, sprinkle cinnamon powder on top before serving for extra flavor.

name: Bienmesabe (Venezuelan-Mexican Sweet)
description: Bienmesabe is a traditional Venezuelan and Mexican sweet dessert made with coconut, sugar, and cinnamon, resulting in a creamy, caramel-flavored treat.
instructions: In a large nonstick pan, toast the grated coconut over medium heat, stirring constantly until golden and fragrant, about 5 minutes.
Add the sugar and continue stirring until the mixture becomes syrupy and caramelized, approximately 3 minutes.
Slowly pour in the coconut milk and add the cinnamon stick, stirring well to combine all ingredients.
Reduce the heat to low and cook the mixture, stirring frequently, until it thickens to a creamy consistency, about 15-20 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and discard the cinnamon stick.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly, then pour into serving dishes.
Optionally, garnish with a sprinkle of ground cinnamon or slivered almonds before serving.

name: Bienmesabe Canario (Canarian Sweet Almond Cream)
description: Bienmesabe Canario is a traditional sweet almond cream dessert from the Canary Islands, known for its rich texture and fragrant flavors of toasted almonds, lemon, and vanilla.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
Spread the almonds evenly on a baking tray and toast them in the oven for about 10 minutes until golden and fragrant. Let them cool.
Grind the toasted almonds into a fine powder using a food processor or blender.
In a saucepan, combine the ground almonds, sugar, and lemon zest with water and bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly.
Reduce the heat and simmer gently for about 20 minutes until the mixture thickens to a creamy consistency, stirring frequently to prevent sticking.
Remove from heat and stir in the beaten egg yolks slowly to incorporate them without curdling.
Return the saucepan to low heat and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring continuously until the cream thickens further.
Remove the lemon zest and vanilla pod if used, then pour the Bienmesabe into serving dishes or a mold.
Allow it to cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving for the flavors to meld and the cream to set.
Serve chilled, optionally garnished with cinnamon or additional ground almonds.

name: Bifana Sandwich
description: Bifana sandwich is a traditional Portuguese sandwich featuring thinly marinated and cooked pork shoulder served in a crusty roll, known for its flavorful and juicy character.
instructions: Combine minced garlic, paprika, white wine, olive oil, salt, and pepper to make a marinade.
Place thinly sliced pork shoulder in the marinade and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight for more flavor.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and cook the marinated pork slices until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Split Portuguese rolls (papo secos) horizontally, but do not cut all the way through to keep them hinged.
Place the cooked pork inside the rolls, allowing some juices to soak into the bread.
Optionally, add mustard or piri-piri sauce to taste for extra flavor.
Serve the bifana sandwich warm, as a traditional Portuguese street food.

name: Bifana in Bread (Portuguese Pork Sandwich)
description: Bifana no Pão is a traditional Portuguese sandwich featuring marinated pork served in a crusty bread roll. It is a flavorful and popular street food in Portugal.
instructions: Slice the pork shoulder into thin pieces or small strips for quick cooking.
Marinate the pork with minced garlic, paprika, white wine, white wine vinegar, bay leaf, and salt. Let it rest for at least 1 hour, preferably overnight in the refrigerator.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the marinated pork pieces to the skillet and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the bay leaf from the cooked pork mixture.
Slice the Portuguese bread rolls horizontally, not cutting all the way through, keeping a hinge.
Fill the bread rolls generously with the cooked pork and some of the pan juices.
Optionally, add mustard or piri-piri sauce to taste before serving.
Serve the Bifana sandwiches hot, ideally accompanied by fries or a salad.

name: Bifteki Me Feta (Feta-stuffed Burger)
description: Bifteki me Feta is a traditional Greek dish featuring ground beef patties stuffed with tangy feta cheese, offering a delicious and savory flavor perfect for a main meal.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground beef, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped parsley, bread crumbs, egg, salt, and black pepper.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly until well combined.
Divide the mixture into equal portions depending on the number of servings.
Take one portion and flatten it into a patty shape on your palm.
Place a cube or spoonful of feta cheese in the center of the patty.
Take another portion of the meat mixture and flatten it, then cover the feta with this second patty, sealing the edges well to encase the cheese inside.
Heat olive oil in a grill pan or skillet over medium heat.
Cook the stuffed burgers for about 5-7 minutes on each side, or until they are cooked through and golden brown on the outside.
Remove from heat and let rest for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the bifteki me feta with a side of Greek salad, pita bread, or tzatziki sauce.

name: Biftekia (Greek Hamburgers)
description: Biftekia are traditional Greek hamburgers made from seasoned ground beef mixed with herbs and spices, grilled to perfection. They are a flavorful and popular Greek dish often served with pita and tzatziki.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground beef, grated onion, minced garlic, chopped parsley, dried oregano, ground cinnamon, ground allspice, salt, and pepper.
Mix the ingredients thoroughly until well combined, using your hands for best results.
Shape the mixture into oval or round patties, about the size of your palm.
Preheat a grill or skillet over medium-high heat and lightly oil the surface with olive oil.
Cook the biftekia patties for about 5-6 minutes on each side, or until nicely browned on the outside and cooked through on the inside.
Serve hot with pita bread, tzatziki sauce, and a side salad or roasted potatoes.

name: Bigarade Sauce (Bitter Orange Sauce)
description: Bigarade Sauce is a classic French sauce made with bitter orange juice, vinegar, and butter, traditionally served with duck. Its balanced sweet and sour flavors complement roasted meats beautifully.
instructions: Combine orange juice, lemon juice, and vinegar in a small saucepan over medium heat and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add sugar, salt, and pepper, and stir until sugar dissolves completely.
In a separate small saucepan, melt butter over low heat.
Whisk the butter gradually into the citrus mixture to emulsify the sauce, stirring constantly to combine.
Continue to simmer the sauce gently until it thickens slightly, about 5 minutes.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt or sugar if needed.
Strain the sauce through a fine mesh sieve to remove any solids for a smooth finish.
Serve warm over duck or other roasted poultry.

name: Bigoli in Salsa (Bigoli Pasta with Onion and Anchovy Sauce)
description: Bigoli in Salsa is a traditional Venetian dish featuring thick bigoli pasta served with a savory onion and anchovy sauce. This simple yet flavorful recipe highlights the local anchovies and sweet onions.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the bigoli pasta until al dente according to the package instructions.
While the pasta cooks, peel and finely slice the onion.
In a large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat and add the sliced onion.
Cook the onion gently until it becomes soft and translucent, about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally to prevent browning.
Add the anchovy fillets to the pan and stir until they dissolve into the onion and oil, forming a sauce.
Drain the cooked pasta, reserving some pasta water.
Add the drained pasta to the pan with the sauce and toss to combine.
If needed, add a little reserved pasta water to loosen the sauce and help it coat the pasta evenly.
Season with freshly ground black pepper to taste and mix well.
Serve immediately, optionally garnished with chopped fresh parsley.

name: Bilecik Chard Rolls
description: Bilecik Chard Rolls are a traditional Turkish stuffed vegetable dish featuring tender chard leaves filled with a seasoned rice mixture, gently simmered to perfection. This vegan and gluten-free specialty is flavored with fresh herbs, olive oil, and lemon juice, offering a light and flavorful meal or appetizer.
instructions: Rinse and boil the chard leaves in salted water until tender, then drain and set aside to cool.
In a large bowl, combine rice, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, chopped dill, and parsley.
Add olive oil, salt, pepper, and lemon juice to the rice mixture, then mix thoroughly.
Place a chard leaf on a clean working surface, put a spoonful of the rice mixture near the stem end.
Roll the leaf tightly around the filling, folding in the sides to form a neat parcel.
Arrange the rolled chard parcels seam side down in a pot, packing them close together.
Drizzle olive oil and lemon juice over the rolls, then add water to just cover them.
Place a heatproof plate on top of the rolls to keep them submerged, cover the pot with a lid.
Simmer gently on low heat for about 40 minutes, until the rice is cooked through.
Allow to cool before serving, garnish with lemon wedges if desired.

name: Bilecik Eggplant Kebab
description: Bilecik Eggplant Kebab is a traditional Turkish dish from the Bilecik region featuring skewered eggplant and seasoned ground lamb, grilled to perfection for a flavorful meal.
instructions: Peel the eggplants and cut them into large chunks.
Salt the eggplant pieces and let them sit for 20 minutes to remove bitterness, then rinse and pat dry.
Chop the onion finely and sauté in olive oil until translucent.
Add the ground lamb to the onions and cook until browned.
Dice the tomatoes and add them to the meat mixture along with salt, pepper, and paprika; cook until the mixture thickens.
Skewer the eggplant pieces alternately with the meat mixture onto metal or wooden skewers.
Grill the skewers on medium heat, turning occasionally, until the eggplants are tender and meat is fully cooked.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley and accompanied by rice or flatbread.

name: Bingol Cheese Specialty
description: Bingol cheese is a traditional Turkish cheese known for its distinct taste and texture, typically served fresh as part of a breakfast or appetizer spread.
instructions: Cut the Bingol cheese into small cubes or slices depending on preference.
If desired, lightly heat the cheese cubes in a skillet over low heat to soften slightly but not melt.
Serve Bingol cheese at room temperature with fresh bread, olives, and tomatoes as a traditional accompaniment.
Optionally, drizzle with a little olive oil or sprinkle with dried oregano to enhance flavor.
Enjoy the cheese as part of a breakfast platter or as an appetizer.

name: Bingol Cut Noodle Soup
description: Bingol Cut Noodle Soup is a traditional Turkish soup from the Bingol region, featuring hand-cut noodles cooked in a creamy, tangy broth flavored with yogurt and lemon juice.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the butter over medium heat until melted.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent and fragrant.
Add the flour to the pot, stirring continuously to form a roux, cook for a few minutes until slightly golden.
Gradually whisk in the chicken broth to prevent lumps, cooking until the mixture thickens slightly.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
In a separate bowl, mix the egg, yogurt, and lemon juice until smooth.
Slowly add some hot soup broth into the yogurt mixture to temper it, then gradually pour this mixture back into the pot, stirring continuously to avoid curdling.
Add the cut noodles (kesme) to the soup and cook until they are tender, about 10-15 minutes.
Optionally, sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley before serving.

name: Birria De Barbacoa from Aguascalientes
description: Birria de Barbacoa from Aguascalientes is a traditional Mexican stew made from marinated beef cooked slowly until tender, known for its rich, chili-infused flavor. This regional variation features a hearty broth and is typically served with warm corn tortillas, fresh onion, cilantro, and lime.
instructions: Soak dried guajillo, ancho, and pasilla chiles in hot water for about 15 minutes until softened.
In a blender, combine the softened chiles, garlic, oregano, cumin, cloves, cinnamon, vinegar, and a pinch of salt; blend until smooth to form the marinade.
Cut the beef chuck and short ribs into large chunks and marinate them with the chile sauce for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight in the refrigerator.
Preheat a large pot and add some vegetable oil; sear the marinated meat pieces on all sides until browned.
Add chopped onion, bay leaves, and beef broth to the pot; cover and simmer gently for about 3 hours until the meat is very tender and shreddable.
Remove the meat from the pot and shred it finely, discarding any bones or excess fat.
Return the shredded meat to the pot and simmer for another 15 minutes to absorb the flavors.
Prepare corn tortillas by warming them on a griddle until pliable.
Serve the birria hot with chopped onion, chopped cilantro, lime wedges, and warm tortillas on the side for making tacos.
Optionally, serve with a bowl of the flavorful broth (consommé) for dipping or sipping.

name: Birria De Borrego (Lamb Birria)
description: Birria de Borrego is a traditional Mexican stew made with marinated lamb slow-cooked in a rich, smoky chili sauce, known for its tender meat and deep flavors. It is often enjoyed as a hearty main dish or flavorful taco filling.
instructions: Toast guajillo, pasilla, and ancho chilies in a dry skillet until fragrant. Remove seeds and stems, then soak chilies in hot water for 20 minutes until softened. In a blender, combine soaked chilies, garlic, white vinegar, cumin, oregano, cinnamon, cloves, salt, pepper, and the soaking water, then blend until smooth to make the marinade.

Cut lamb shoulder into large chunks and place in a large bowl. Pour the chili marinade over the lamb, ensuring pieces are well coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight, to marinate.

Preheat oven to 160°C (320°F). In a heavy oven-safe pot or Dutch oven, heat vegetable oil over medium-high heat. Remove lamb pieces from marinade, reserving the marinade. Brown lamb pieces on all sides, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add sliced onion and minced garlic, cooking until translucent. Return lamb to the pot and pour reserved marinade over it along with beef broth to cover the meat.
Cover the pot with a lid or foil and transfer to the oven. Cook for 3 hours or until the lamb is very tender and shreds easily.

Remove the lamb from the pot and shred the meat using two forks. Strain the cooking liquid to remove solids and return the liquid to the pot. Simmer the liquid to reduce and concentrate flavors for about 15 minutes.
Add shredded lamb back to the pot with the reduced sauce and stir to combine. Adjust seasoning with salt if needed.
Serve the birria hot in bowls with chopped onion, cilantro, and lime wedges. It can also be served as a filling for tacos along with the broth (consomme).

name: Birria De Chivo (Goat Birria Stew)
description: Birria de Chivo is a traditional Mexican stew made with tender goat meat simmered in a rich, flavorful chili sauce. This dish is celebrated for its deep, smoky flavors and hearty texture, often served with fresh garnishes and tortillas.
instructions: Soak guajillo, ancho, and pasilla chili peppers in hot water for 15 minutes until soft, then drain and blend with garlic, cumin, oregano, vinegar, salt, and a little water to form a smooth marinade.
Cut the goat meat into large chunks and marinate it with the chili sauce overnight or at least 4 hours in the refrigerator.
In a large pot, heat some vegetable oil and sear the marinated goat meat until browned on all sides.
Add chopped onion, bay leaf, and enough water to cover the meat.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for about 3 hours or until the goat meat is very tender and falls apart.
Remove the meat from the broth and shred it with forks.
Strain the broth to remove solids and return it to the pot.
Add the shredded meat back into the broth and heat through.
Serve the birria hot in bowls, garnished with chopped onion, cilantro, lime wedges, and warm corn tortillas on the side.

name: Birria De Res (Beef Birria Stew)
description: Birria de Res is a traditional Mexican beef stew cooked with a rich blend of dried chiles and spices, resulting in a deeply flavorful and tender meat dish often served with fresh garnishes and corn tortillas.
instructions: Prepare the dried chiles by removing stems and seeds, then soak them in hot water for 20 minutes until softened.
In a blender, combine soaked chiles, garlic, white vinegar, cumin, oregano, cloves, cinnamon, black pepper, and salt to create the marinade sauce.
Cut the beef into large chunks and coat thoroughly with the marinade. Let it marinate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight in the refrigerator.
In a large pot, heat some vegetable oil over medium heat and brown the marinated beef pieces on all sides to seal in the flavors.

Add chopped onion, bay leaves, and enough beef broth to cover the meat. Bring to a boil, then reduce to simmer and cook covered for about 3 hours or until the beef is tender and can be shredded easily.
Once cooked, remove the beef and shred it with forks. Strain the cooking liquid to remove solids and return the liquid to the pot.
Add the shredded beef back into the pot with the broth and keep warm.
To serve, ladle the stew into bowls and garnish with chopped onion, fresh cilantro, and lime wedges. Optionally, serve with warm corn tortillas and chopped radish on the side.

name: Birria Tacos American Style
description: American-style birria tacos feature slow-cooked, marinated beef served in tortillas with melted cheese, accompanied by a flavorful dipping broth called consommé, combining traditional Mexican flavors with a popular American twist.
instructions: Combine dried guajillo and ancho chili peppers with garlic, cumin, oregano, cloves, cinnamon, and bay leaf in a bowl and cover with hot water. Let soak until soft, then blend with white vinegar and water to create a smooth marinade.
Cut beef chuck into large chunks and marinate with the chili sauce for at least two hours, preferably overnight, in the refrigerator.
Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Place marinated beef and sauce in a heavy oven-safe pot or Dutch oven, add beef broth, and bring to a simmer on stove.
Cover and transfer to the oven, cooking slowly for about 3 hours until the meat is very tender and shreds easily.
Once cooked, shred the beef and keep warm in the sauce.

Heat a skillet over medium heat and dip corn tortillas briefly in the birria sauce, then cook on the skillet, add shredded cheese, and cooked beef, fold the tortilla and cook until crispy and cheese melts.
Serve the tacos hot with chopped onion, cilantro, lime wedges, and a side of consommé made from the cooking broth for dipping.

name: Biscotti Al Burro (Butter Cookies)
description: Biscotti al Burro are traditional Italian butter cookies known for their rich, buttery flavor and crisp texture. Simple to prepare, they make a delightful snack or accompaniment to coffee or tea.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a bowl, cream the butter and sugar together until light and fluffy using a mixer or wooden spoon.
Add the egg and vanilla extract to the butter mixture and mix until fully combined.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt.
Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients and mix until a soft dough forms.
Using a spoon or your hands, form small balls of dough (about 25 g each) and place them spaced apart on the prepared baking sheet.
Flatten each ball gently with the palm of your hand to form a thick cookie shape.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 12-15 minutes or until the edges are lightly golden.
Remove the cookies from the oven and allow them to cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

name: Biscotti Di Prato (Cantucci)
description: Biscotti di Prato, also known as Cantucci, are traditional crunchy Italian almond biscuits originating from Tuscany, perfect for dipping in coffee or Vin Santo wine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a large bowl, whisk together the sugar and eggs until the mixture is thick and pale.
Add the vanilla extract and mix well.
Gradually add the flour, baking powder, and salt to the egg mixture, stirring to combine into a dough.
Fold in the sliced almond until evenly distributed.
Turn the dough onto a floured surface and shape it into a log approximately 30 cm long and 6 cm wide.
Place the log on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 25-30 minutes until lightly golden and firm to the touch.
Remove the log from the oven and let it cool for about 10 minutes to firm up slightly.
Reduce the oven temperature to 160°C (320°F).
Slice the log diagonally into 1 cm thick slices using a serrated knife.
Arrange the slices cut side down on the baking sheet and bake for an additional 10-15 minutes, turning them halfway through, until they are dry and crisp.
Let the biscotti cool completely on a wire rack before serving.

name: Biscotto Gelato (Ice Cream Sandwich)
description: Biscotto Gelato is a classic Italian ice cream sandwich made by sandwiching creamy vanilla ice cream between two crisp almond biscotti, offering a delightful contrast of textures.
instructions: Soften the ice cream by leaving it out of the freezer for a few minutes until it is spreadable but not melted.
Place one biscotto (Italian almond biscuit) on a flat surface.
Spoon a generous amount of softened ice cream onto the biscotto.
Top with a second biscotto, pressing gently to spread the ice cream evenly between the biscuits.
Wrap the assembled ice cream sandwich in plastic wrap and place it in the freezer for at least 1 hour to firm up before serving.
Serve chilled as a refreshing dessert.

name: Biscuit Layer Cake
description: Biscuit Layer Cake is a simple Turkish dessert made by layering tea biscuits with creamy custard, chilled until set for a deliciously soft and sweet treat.
instructions: Line the bottom of a rectangular or square serving dish with a layer of plain tea biscuits, fitting them closely together.
In a saucepan, combine milk, sugar, and flour, stirring until the flour dissolves completely.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and cook the mixture, stirring constantly, until it thickens to a pudding-like consistency.
Remove the saucepan from heat and stir in vanilla extract for flavor.
Pour half of the prepared custard over the biscuit layer, spreading it evenly.
Add another layer of biscuits on top of the custard, arranging them neatly.
Pour the remaining custard over the second biscuit layer, smoothing the surface.
Cover the dish with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours or until the cake sets properly.
Before serving, optionally sprinkle cocoa powder or grated chocolate on top for decoration.

name: Biscuits and Gravy
description: Biscuits and gravy is a classic Southern American breakfast dish featuring soft, flaky biscuits smothered in rich, creamy sausage gravy.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 220°C (425°F).
In a large bowl, combine biscuit mix and milk until just blended; do not overmix.
Drop biscuit dough onto an ungreased baking sheet in spoonfuls.
Bake biscuits for 10-12 minutes until golden brown and cooked through.
While biscuits bake, heat a large skillet over medium heat and add sausage, breaking it up with a spoon.
Cook sausage until browned and cooked completely, about 5-7 minutes.
Sprinkle flour over the sausage and stir to coat.
Slowly pour in milk while stirring constantly to prevent lumps, cooking until the gravy thickens, about 3-5 minutes.
Season gravy with salt and black pepper to taste.
Split warm biscuits in half and spoon sausage gravy generously over each biscuit half.
Serve immediately for best taste.

name: Bison Burger
description: A bison burger is a flavorful, lean alternative to traditional beef burgers, made with ground bison meat and topped with classic burger condiments.
instructions: Preheat the grill or skillet to medium-high heat.
In a bowl, gently mix ground bison with salt and pepper; avoid overmixing to keep the burger tender.
Form the mixture into patties about 1 inch thick and slightly larger than the bun to account for shrinkage during cooking.
Lightly oil the grill grates or skillet to prevent sticking.
Cook the bison patties for about 4-5 minutes per side for medium-rare, or longer if desired, flipping once.
During the last minute of cooking, place a slice of cheese on each patty to melt, if using.
Toast the burger buns lightly on the grill or skillet until golden brown.
Assemble the burger by placing the cooked patty on the bottom bun, then add desired toppings like lettuce, tomato, onion, pickles, ketchup, mustard, and mayonnaise.
Cover with the top bun and serve immediately.

name: Bistec Encebollado (Steak with Onions)
description: Bistec Encebollado is a traditional Latin American dish featuring tender beef steak marinated and cooked with sautéed onions, offering a flavorful and comforting meal.
instructions: Marinate the steak pieces with garlic, salt, pepper, and vinegar for at least 30 minutes.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Remove the steak from the marinade and cook in the hot oil until browned on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side depending on thickness.
Remove the steak from the skillet and set aside, keeping warm.
In the same skillet, add sliced onion and cook until translucent and slightly caramelized, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the remaining marinade to the skillet with onions and simmer for a couple of minutes to combine flavors.
Return the steak to the skillet with onions and cook together for another 2 minutes to warm through and meld the flavors.
Serve the bistec topped with the sautéed onions and sauce, accompanied by rice or fried plantains if desired.

name: Bistec Ranchero (Ranch-style Steak)
description: Bistec Ranchero is a traditional Mexican dish featuring a pan-seared seasoned steak served with a fresh tomato-onion-chili sauce, embodying bold flavors typical of ranch-style cooking.
instructions: Pat the steak dry with paper towels and season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the steak to the skillet and cook for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked to your desired doneness.
Remove the steak from the skillet and set aside to rest.
In the same skillet, add the chopped onion and garlic, sauté until softened and fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the diced tomato, chopped jalapeno, and chopped cilantro to the skillet and cook for another 3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Return the steak to the skillet and spoon the sauce over the top.
Simmer everything together for 2 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Serve the ranch-style steak hot, garnished with extra cilantro if desired.

name: Bistec a La Mexicana (Mexican-style Steak)
description: Bistec a la Mexicana is a traditional Mexican dish featuring tender steak strips cooked with fresh tomatoes, onion, jalapeno, and cilantro for a flavorful and colorful meal.
instructions: Slice the steak into thin strips for quicker cooking and better absorption of flavors.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until translucent.
Add the chopped tomato and jalapeno, cooking until softened.
Add the sliced steak to the skillet and cook until browned on all sides.
Season with salt and pepper and continue cooking until steak is cooked through.
Add chopped cilantro at the end, stirring to combine.
Serve hot with warm tortillas and optional sides such as rice or beans.

name: Bistec in Pasilla Chile Sauce
description: Bistec en Salsa de Chile Pasilla is a traditional Mexican dish featuring tender beef steak simmered in a rich and flavorful pasilla chile sauce, offering a balanced heat and smoky taste.
instructions: Rinse the beef steak and pat it dry with paper towels.
Season both sides of the steak with salt and pepper.
Heat the vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the steak to the skillet and sear for about 4 minutes on each side or until cooked to your preferred level of doneness.
Remove the steak from the skillet and keep warm.
In the same skillet, add the sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Pour in the pasilla chile sauce and stir to combine with the onions and garlic.
Simmer the sauce for 5 minutes to heat through and meld flavors.
Return the steak to the skillet and spoon the sauce over it.
Cook together for another 2-3 minutes to allow the steak to absorb some of the sauce.
Serve the bistec topped with the pasilla chile sauce, garnished with fresh cilantro if desired.

name: Bistecca Alla Fiorentina (Florentine Steak)
description: Bistecca alla Fiorentina is a traditional Tuscan grilled T-bone steak known for its simplicity, quality of meat, and preparation over high heat to produce a rare, juicy interior with a crisp exterior.
instructions: Take a thick T-bone steak (at least 4 cm thick) and bring it to room temperature, about 30-40 minutes before cooking.
Lightly brush both sides of the steak with olive oil and season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Preheat a charcoal grill or heavy grill pan to very high heat until it’s smoking hot.
Place the steak on the grill and sear for about 5-6 minutes without moving it, until a good crust forms.
Flip the steak and sear the other side for an additional 5-6 minutes.
Check the internal temperature or doneness: it should be rare to medium-rare (about 50-55°C) internally.
Remove the steak from the grill and let it rest for about 5-10 minutes to allow the juices to redistribute.
Optionally, squeeze a little lemon juice over the steak before serving.
Slice the steak across the bone into thick slices and serve immediately, typically with a drizzle of high-quality olive oil and crisp Tuscan bread on the side.

name: Bitlis Bulgur Pilaf
description: Bitlis Bulgur Pilaf is a traditional Turkish bulgur pilaf dish from Bitlis province, characterized by its simple preparation with tomatoes and onions, resulting in a flavorful and hearty side or main dish.
instructions: Rinse the bulgur in cold water and drain well.
Heat the butter and olive oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent.
Add diced tomato and cook for a few minutes until softened.
Stir in the bulgur and toast it for 2-3 minutes, mixing well with the vegetables.
Add hot water or chicken broth and salt, then stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for about 15-20 minutes until the bulgur absorbs all the liquid and becomes tender.
Turn off the heat and let the pilaf rest covered for 10 minutes.
Fluff the bulgur pilaf with a fork before serving.

name: Bitlis Büryan (Bitlis Style Pit Roast Lamb)
description: Bitlis Büryan is a traditional Kurdish-style pit-roasted lamb dish from Bitlis, Turkey, where lamb is slow-cooked in a heated earth pit until tender and flavorful.
instructions: First, prepare the lamb by cleaning and cutting it into large pieces suitable for roasting.
Season the lamb pieces generously with salt, black pepper, and ground cumin.
Marinate the seasoned lamb with garlic and a little vegetable oil, allowing it to rest for about one hour.
While the lamb marinates, prepare a traditional earth pit by digging a hole in the ground with a depth of about 60 cm (two feet).
Line the pit with stones and preheat them by building a fire inside the pit; let the stones heat up thoroughly over about two hours.
Once the stones are hot, remove the embers and ashes from the pit, leaving the heated stones at the bottom.
Place the marinated lamb pieces wrapped in banana leaves or salt-proof foil inside the pit on the hot stones.
Cover the pit tightly with a lid or a thick layer of soil and let the lamb cook slowly using the residual heat for about 3 to 4 hours.
After cooking, carefully remove the lamb from the pit and unwrap it.
Serve the lamb hot with traditional accompaniments like flatbread, grilled vegetables, and fresh herbs.

name: Bizcochada (Sponge Cake Soaked)
description: Bizcochada is a traditional Spanish-style sponge cake soaked in cinnamon-infused sweetened milk, making it moist and flavorful.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 180°C (350°F).
In a large bowl, beat the egg yolks with sugar until the mixture is pale and creamy.
In a separate bowl, whisk the egg whites until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold the whipped egg whites into the egg yolk mixture, taking care not to deflate the batter.
Sift the flour over the mixture and gently incorporate it with a spatula.
Pour the batter into a buttered and floured baking pan and bake for about 25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove the sponge cake from the oven and allow it to cool slightly.
In a saucepan, combine milk, cinnamon stick, and sugar and bring to a gentle boil, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
Remove from heat and allow the milk mixture to cool and infuse the cinnamon flavor.
Once the cake has cooled, poke multiple holes all over the surface with a skewer or fork.
Slowly pour the cooled milk mixture over the cake, allowing it to soak thoroughly.
Refrigerate the cake for at least 2 hours to absorb the milk mixture.
Before serving, dust the top with powdered sugar and cinnamon if desired.

name: Black Bean Soup
description: Black Bean Soup is a hearty, flavorful dish made with simmered black beans and aromatic spices, originating from Latin American cuisine. It is commonly enjoyed as a nutritious and comforting soup.
instructions: Rinse the black beans thoroughly under cold water and soak them overnight in enough water to cover.
Drain the soaked beans and place them in a large pot with fresh water covering by about two inches.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer and cook the beans for about 1 to 1.5 hours or until tender.
While the beans cook, heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, and diced green bell pepper to the skillet and sauté until the vegetables are soft and translucent.
Add ground cumin, chili powder, and smoked paprika to the vegetables and cook for another minute to release their aroma.
When the beans are tender, add the sautéed vegetables and spices to the pot.
Stir in salt and black pepper to taste and simmer the soup for another 20 minutes to meld flavors.
For a smoother texture, use an immersion blender to partially puree the soup, leaving some beans whole for texture.
Remove from heat and stir in freshly chopped cilantro and lime juice.
Serve the soup hot, optionally garnished with sour cream, avocado slices, or additional cilantro if desired.

name: Black Drip Coffee
description: Black drip coffee is a simple, pure brew made by dripping hot water over ground coffee in a filter, producing a clean, aromatic cup without additives.
instructions: Heat fresh water to around 90-96 degrees Celsius (just off boiling).
Place a drip coffee filter cone over a coffee cup or carafe.
Add medium-ground coffee into the filter, using about 15 grams of coffee per 250 ml of water.
Slowly pour a small amount of hot water over the grounds to let them bloom for about 30 seconds.
Gradually pour the remaining hot water in a steady spiral over the coffee grounds, allowing the brewed coffee to drip through the filter.
Once the dripping slows and the desired amount of coffee has brewed, remove the filter cone.
Serve the black drip coffee hot.

name: Black Eyed Peas for New Year's
description: Traditional southern black eyed peas cooked with smoked meats and seasonings, eaten on New Year's Day for good luck and prosperity.
instructions: Rinse the black eyed peas thoroughly under cold water and sort through them to remove any debris or damaged peas.
In a large pot, add the rinsed black eyed peas and cover with water, about 2 inches above the peas.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, sliced smoked sausage, and chopped smoked ham for flavor.
Season with salt, black pepper, and a pinch of cayenne pepper for a little heat.
Bring the pot to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat and simmer uncovered for about 1 to 1.5 hours or until the peas are tender.
Check the water level occasionally and add more if necessary to keep the peas submerged.
Once the peas are tender and the flavors melded, adjust seasoning if needed.
If desired, stir in chopped fresh parsley just before serving.
Serve the black eyed peas hot as a main dish or side, traditionally eaten on New Year's Day for good luck.

name: Black Jelly Drink Nam Chao Kuay
description: Black Jelly Drink Nam Chao Kuay is a popular refreshing Asian beverage featuring sweetened black herbal jelly served chilled in syrup. It's a cooling traditional drink enjoyed especially in warm climates.
instructions: Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan.
Add 50 grams of black jelly powder to the boiling water, stirring continuously to dissolve completely.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Remove from heat and let the jelly mixture cool to room temperature.
Pour the cooled jelly mixture into a large container and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until set.
In a separate bowl, mix 500 ml of cold water with 100 grams of sugar until fully dissolved to make the syrup.
Cut the set black jelly into small cubes.
Fill serving glasses halfway with black jelly cubes.
Add syrup to the glasses according to sweetness preference.
Top off each glass with cold water or ice cubes if desired.
Stir gently before serving to combine the jelly and syrup well.

name: Black Olive Tapenade
description: Black olive tapenade is a flavorful Provençal spread made from finely chopped black olives, capers, and herbs, perfect as an appetizer or condiment.
instructions: Rinse the black olives under cold water and drain well.
Place the olives, minced garlic, capers, lemon juice, and chopped parsley in a food processor.
Pulse the mixture until coarsely chopped but not completely pureed, maintaining some texture.
With the processor running, slowly add olive oil until the tapenade reaches a spreadable consistency.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste, then pulse briefly to combine.
Transfer to a bowl, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Serve the tapenade spread on crusty bread or as a condiment with grilled meats or vegetables.

name: Black Pork Loin Alentejo Style
description: Black Pork Loin Alentejo Style is a traditional Portuguese dish from the Alentejo region, featuring tender pork loin braised in a savory sauce of white wine, paprika, and herbs, served with potatoes.
instructions: Cut the black pork loin into thick slices and marinate with garlic, white wine, paprika, bay leaf, salt, and pepper for at least 2 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.
Heat olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat and brown the pork slices on all sides until golden but not completely cooked through.
Remove the pork and set aside. In the same skillet, sauté sliced onions until translucent, then add chopped garlic and cook for another minute.
Add diced tomato and cook until softened. Stir in paprika and bay leaf, then return the pork to the skillet.
Pour in white wine and enough water to cover the pork halfway. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook gently for about 45 minutes until pork is tender and the sauce has thickened.
In the last 10 minutes of cooking, add diced potatoes and cook until tender.
Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Remove bay leaf and garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

name: Black Pork Ribs with Migas
description: This dish features tender black pork ribs slow-cooked until succulent, served alongside crispy migas—traditional toasted bread crumbs seasoned with garlic and smoked paprika for a flavorful Spanish-inspired meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 160°C (320°F).
Season 800 g of black pork ribs generously with salt and pepper.
Heat 2 tbsp of olive oil in a large ovenproof skillet over medium heat.
Brown the ribs on all sides until golden, then transfer the skillet to the oven and cook for 1.5 hours until tender.
Meanwhile, prepare the migas by heating 3 tbsp of olive oil in a separate large pan over medium heat.
Add 4 cloves of chopped garlic and sauté until fragrant but not browned.
Add 200 g of day-old bread torn into small pieces and cook, stirring frequently, until the bread is toasted and crispy.
Mix in 1 tsp of smoked paprika and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Once the ribs are done, remove them from the oven and let them rest for 10 minutes.
Serve the ribs on a plate with the toasted migas on the side, garnished with freshly chopped parsley.

name: Black Pork Secretos Grilled
description: Black Pork Secretos is a prized cut from Iberian pigs, known for its rich marbling and tenderness. Grilled simply with salt and pepper, it offers an intensely flavorful and juicy experience.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
Lightly season the black pork secretos with salt and freshly ground black pepper on both sides.
Brush the grill grates with a little olive oil to prevent sticking.
Place the secretos on the grill and cook for about 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare, or longer depending on desired doneness.
Remove the pork from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes to allow the juices to redistribute.
Slice the secretos against the grain into thin strips.
Serve immediately, optionally garnished with fresh chopped parsley or a squeeze of lemon.

name: Black Pork Tenderloin with Acorn Sauce
description: This dish features tender black pork tenderloin paired with a creamy acorn sauce, offering a unique nutty flavor and a rich complement to the pork. The acorn sauce is made by simmering and puréeing acorns with onion, wine, and cream for a smooth, flavorful accompaniment.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (390°F).
Season the black pork tenderloin with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in an ovenproof skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the tenderloin on all sides until browned, about 2 minutes per side.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and roast the pork for 15-20 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 63°C (145°F). Remove pork from oven and let rest for 5 minutes.
While the pork rests, prepare the acorn sauce.
In a saucepan, melt butter over medium heat.
Add finely chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add chopped acorn pieces and toast lightly for 2-3 minutes.
Pour in white wine and cook until reduced by half.
Add chicken broth and simmer until acorns are tender, about 15 minutes.
Purée the sauce mixture using a blender or immersion blender until smooth.
Return the puréed sauce to the pan, stir in cream, and simmer until slightly thickened.
Season the sauce with salt and pepper to taste.
Slice the rested pork tenderloin and serve with the warm acorn sauce poured over.

name: Black Pudding and Apple Galette
description: A rustic French-style galette combining rich and savory black pudding with sweet, spiced apple slices wrapped in buttery puff pastry. This dish offers a unique sweet and savory flavor contrast perfect for any meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F).
Roll out the puff pastry on a floured surface into a rough circle about 12 inches in diameter.
Peel, core, and slice the apple thinly and toss with lemon juice, cinnamon, and a tablespoon of sugar.
Remove the casing from the black pudding and crumble it into small pieces.
Spread a thin layer of Dijon mustard over the center of the pastry, leaving about a 2-inch border.
Arrange the apple slices evenly over the mustard, then scatter the black pudding pieces on top.
Fold the edges of the pastry over the filling, pleating to make a circular enclosure and leaving the center exposed.
Brush the pastry edges with beaten egg to achieve a golden color during baking.
Sprinkle sugar over the apples and folded pastry edges for extra caramelization.
Bake the galette for 30-35 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and crisp, and the apples are tender.
Allow to cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving warm.

name: Black Pudding with Apples
description: A traditional British dish featuring crispy black pudding served with sweet, caramelized apples, balancing savory and fruity flavors.
instructions: Slice the black pudding into 1 cm thick slices.
Peel, core, and thinly slice the apples.
Heat a frying pan over medium heat and add a tablespoon of vegetable oil.
Fry the black pudding slices for about 3-4 minutes on each side until crispy and cooked through, then remove them from the pan and keep warm.
